name: Nelder-Mead Simplex
key_facts: Nelder-Mead is a direct search method that does not require gradient computation
It operates on a simplex (a polytope with n+1 vertices in n dimensions)
Key operations include reflection, expansion, contraction, and shrinkage
The algorithm was proposed by John Nelder and Roger Mead in 1965
It is commonly used for low-dimensional optimization problems
Nelder-Mead does not guarantee convergence to a local minimum in general
The method is also known as the downhill simplex method or amoeba method
forbidden_claims: Nelder-Mead guarantees convergence to the global optimum
Nelder-Mead is efficient in high-dimensional spaces
Nelder-Mead is a linear programming algorithm (it is not the Simplex method for LP)
